First Kidnapping Attempt at Camp is Foiled
It was bound to happen one day. For years,
tension has mounted as parents and campers prepare
toast, bacon, cereal, juice and milk
to part ways at Choral Camp early on Monday
Lunch: Sloppy joes, vegetables with dip,
afternoon. This year, the tension for one Dad
frozen grapes, milk, ice cream bars
apparently became too much. Although he had
brought his daughter all the way from Nashville, as
green beans, rolls, peaches, milk, cookies
the time to leave her approached, he wrapped his
arms tightly around her while fixing his face with a
friendly smile to ward off suspicion.
Eager to join the fun of camp, his daughter
struggled against her father’s powerful embrace, 8:00 Breakfast
